task_yaml.task:
I'm preparing a scientific figure with 5 data series (a multi-line plot) for
a journal. It needs to be readable for red-green colorblind reviewers AND it
will sometimes be printed in black and white, so the colors must also stay
distinguishable in grayscale. Help me pick the best 5-color palette from a
fixed set of candidates.

The candidate palettes are provided offline at:

    /tmp_workspace/clawbench/sources/palettes.json

Each palette has 5 colors as RGB triples and hex (ColorBrewer qualitative
schemes, a jet/rainbow sampling, Okabe-Ito, and viridis). Evaluate them
objectively — don't go by reputation. Compute, for each palette:

- HARD constraints (must pass ALL):
  1. Red-green colorblind safety: simulate deuteranopia AND protanopia, and
     require the minimum pairwise perceptual difference (deltaE) among the 5
     colors to be at least 15 under both.
  2. Grayscale safety: when reduced to grayscale luminance, the minimum
     pairwise luminance separation must be at least 5 (on a 0-100 scale), so
     the series stay distinct in black-and-white print.

- SOFT preferences (tie-break among hard-constraint survivors): prefer larger
  worst-case red-green separation, then larger grayscale separation.

Pick exactly ONE palette and justify with the computed numbers. Be careful:
a colorful-looking palette can be useless in grayscale, and a famous
"colorblind-safe" palette can still fail the grayscale/print requirement.

Save:
- `/tmp_workspace/results/palette_choice.json`: your pick (`selected_id` plus
  the metrics justifying it), the list of palettes passing all hard
  constraints, and the tie-break reasoning.
- `/tmp_workspace/results/palette_evaluation.json`: per-palette computed
  metrics (worst-case red-green deltaE under deuteranopia/protanopia, and
  grayscale min separation) with pass/fail and the failing constraint named.
- `/tmp_workspace/results/palette_method.json`: how you simulated color
  blindness, computed deltaE, and computed grayscale luminance (reproducible).

This is fully offline — do not fetch anything from the network.
